title: Muscat Stock Exchange
content:
Muscat Stock Exchange
Listing
Listing
Print
Listing
Benefits
Listing
What are the Benefits to be listed?
Diversifying Funding options: Going public through offering and listing on the stock market is a way for companies to raise capital from new investors, strengthening their financial position. This influx of capital is crucial for fostering business growth and expansion. Additionally, being listed in the Muscat Stock Exchange not only improves companies' financial standing but also makes it easier for them to access bank financing and loans.
Increasing the company’s credibility: Becoming listed in financial markets elevates the company's trustworthiness through the adoption of transparent and governance standards. This, in return, it expands the company's prospects, and opens doors to additional development opportunities such as mergers, acquisitions, and collaborative projects.
Expanding the investor base: Listing in the capital markets allows companies to diversify the investor base and bring in experienced investors who can contribute to developing the company’s business and entering new markets.
Strengthening the company’s brand: Listing in the capital markets offers a platform to showcase companies, their services, and financial performance. Media coverage during the IPO and listing processes, analyst reports, and essential disclosures provide visibility. This visibility, strengthens the company's brand that attracts new customers and investors.
Enhancing corporate governance and sustainability: Ensuring the sustainability of companies beyond the second generation poses a significant challenge, particularly for family businesses. Converting to joint-stock companies and getting listed on the stock exchange offers a legal framework. This not only enhances the likelihood of continued existence but also helps mitigate risks associated with internal or family issues of the founders that could potentially impact the company.
Facilitating the exit process and liquidating shares: The stock exchange serves as a key platform for investors to smoothly exit and liquidate their shares in a company. Through offerings, listings, and trading, investors can easily buy and sell shares, streamlining the overall process.

a) to invest the company's money in different aspects of Industrial& commercail investment and own the commercail and Industrial projects related with selling and producing Polypropylene films on a commercail & Industrial basis in order to gain profit for the subscribers .  b)do all the relevant and asociated works with an aim to sell the product inside Oman and abroad .  c) to purchase or otherwise acquire or takte on leas lands for the purposes of te company and to constrcuct buildings and warehouses and dispose the same by legal ways of conveyacing .  d) to purchase, own,lease and operate the machines, equipments,tools and the necessary out fittings to carry out the company's works .  e) to import,export,purchase and deal with raw materials its products, and derivatives , and to manufacture , sell and deal in these materials .  f) to enter into contract or any other arrangments with the government of the Sultanate of Oman Companies . Pblic- private Establihments & Individuals either inside or outside the Sultanate of Oman for assistance in managing all or part of the company's projects & activities and enter into a contract with experts and consultants and to employ workmen and officails .  g) agencies and Commercial representation.  h) commercial mediation and general import .  i) to obtain shres in any other company which has the same or few of its objectives and enter in any work which may be useful either directly or indirectly rither within or outside the Sultanate of Oman .  j) to invest its surplus monies n the way that seems to suitable by the Board of Directors to obatin the objectts for which the company is established .

Profile
Asyad Shipping, the maritime arm of Asyad Group, has been a key player in global trade since its establishment in 2003. Operating one of the world’s largest and most diversified fleets, Asyad Shipping serves over 60 countries, connecting east and west while driving trade across high-growth markets in Asia, the Middle East, and North Africa. Renowned for its strong partnerships with top-tier international clients, Asyad Shipping has built a legacy of trust, reliability, and customer-centricity. Its leadership and dedicated team have forged long-standing relationships by delivering tailored solutions and staying attuned to the evolving needs of its customers.International Maritime Goods Transport

Profile
Oman Investment and Finance Co. SAOG (Khedmah) is a pioneering and leading organization in the collection of utilities, providing a centralized payment point for customers to pay all their electricity, water, and telecom utility bills. It also facilitates the collection of Social Protection Fund subscriptions and supports public services such as motor insurance renewals, vehicle registration card printing (Mulkiya), and the collection of traffic fines for the Royal Oman Police (ROP). Additionally, the company has investments in the banking, finance, and electricity contracting sectors.

Profile
More
Profile
Launched in 2003 upon the acquisition of British Petroleum Oman, Oman Oil Marketing Company (OOMCO) today operates one of the largest networks of service stations in the Sultanate, along with a host of complementary services that go far beyond fuelling your vehicle. OOMCO boasts 100% Omani ownership, setting it apart in the fuel sector. OQ owns 49% of OOMCO’s shares, while the rest is divided among government authorities, pension funds, shareholders and individuals. Oman Oil Marketing Company (OOMCO) is continuing to expand its global footprint with operations in Saudi Arabia and Tanzania embarking on a journey to achieve strategic growth. OOMCO is a market leader with a market share of over 40% in Oman’s domestic fuel business. OOMCO’s operations involve: • Retail fuels (B2C) • Commercial fuels: direct sales (B2B) • Lubricants • Aviation fuels • Storage and distribution • Marine fuels • Non-fuel business

Profile
The Oman Telecommunications Company (Omantel) is the leading integrated telecommunications services provider and has the widest services with the broadest network coverage in Oman. The services includes fixed voice, broadband, mobile and wholesale services. Omantel is a major international hub in the submarine cable business. Omantel is the first GCC-based carrier to land a submarine cable in Europe. It has submarine investments in 14 major submarine cables systems and operations in 5 separate landing stations. Omantel’s global infrastructure provides direct access connectivity to more than 46 countries in Europe, Asia, Africa, the Middle East and North America. Omantel’s acquisitions in Zain resulted in becoming the second largest shareholder in Zain with 21.9% shareholding.

Profile
Raysut Cement is a leading manufacturer and distributor of cement. The companys product portfolio includes: • Ordinary Portland Cement: Suitable for general construction purposes, including plain and reinforced concrete works, masonry, plastering, and flooring. • Sulfate Resistant Cement: Designed for concrete applications exposed to sulfate-rich environments, such as foundations and sewage treatment plants. • Oil Well Cement (Class G): Used in the oil and gas industry for cementing work in drilling operations. • Pozzolana Well Cement: Utilized for cementing shallow oil wells. The companys main production facility is located in Salalah. Raysut Cement serves both domestic and international markets.

Profile
Financial Holding Companies (MOCI register) As a holding company, as per its Articles of Association and article 127 of the CCL, the objects of the Company are to manage its subsidiary companies (or to participate in the management of other companies in which it holds shares); to invest its funds in shares, bonds and securities; to provide loans, security and finance to its subsidiaries; and to own patents, trademarks, concessions and other incorporeal rights, utilize them and lease them to its subsidiaries and other companies. The Company is a holding company and owns shares in the Project Companies (i.e. SMN Barka and RPC) which own and operate (through STOMO) each of the SMN Barka and Rusail plants. The Project Companies operate both plants through STOMO.
